title: Samsung Galaxy A27 launched in India
description: The Samsung Galaxy A27, unveiled last week, made its India debut today. The smartphone comes in Light Green, Light Pink, and Black colors in India, with three memory configurations - 6GB/128GB, 8GB/128GB, and 8GB/256GB, priced at INR31,999, INR34,999, and INR40,499, respectively. [#InlinePriceWidget,14606,1#] The Galaxy A27 will go on sale in India starting July 3 through Samsung's official website, exclusive outlets, partner stores, and other online platforms. Samsung said all models are eligible for INR3,000 cashback on bank/UPI payments, but didn't reveal for how long. Anyway,...

title: New leak details the cameras on Samsung's upcoming Galaxy Z foldables
description: Samsung is expected to unveil its next generation of Galaxy Z foldables in July. The lineup is rumored to include the Galaxy Z Fold8 wide, Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra, and Galaxy Z Flip8. Ahead of the expected launch, a new leak has revealed the key camera specifications of all three devices. According to the leak, the Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra will retain the Galaxy Z Fold7's triple rear camera setup, but replace the 12MP ultrawide camera with a new 50MP sensor. Dummy units of the Galaxy Z Fold8 wide (left) and Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra (right) The foldable is otherwise expected to keep the same...

title: OnePlus N6 arrives with 8,000mAh battery and Dimensity 6360 Max
description: There’s a brand-new OnePlus smartphone series and India is the first market to get it. The standout feature on the OnePlus N6 is its massive 8,000mAh battery. It supports 45W SuperVOOC wired charging as well as 5W reverse wired charging, so you can use the N6 as a powerbank. OnePlus also incorporated bypass charging, so the N6 can draw power directly to its motherboard without it ever reaching the battery. This helps keep the device cool during intensive tasks like gaming. OnePlus proudly claims the N6’s battery is engineered to survive over 1,600 charge cycles and retain over 80% of...

title: Qualcomm announces Snapdragon Summit 2026 dates
description: Qualcomm has announced it will host its annual Snapdragon Summit from September 22-24 in Maui, Hawaii. While Qualcomm hasn't revealed what it will announce at this Snapdragon Summit, it's likely the US-based chipmaker will introduce a successor to last year's flagship Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 SoC, rumored to be called the Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 6 (SM8950). However, that might not be the only mobile platform Qualcomm will announce at the Summit, as it's rumored to introduce the Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 6 Pro (SM8975) as well. A recent leak revealed that Qualcomm is also working on...

title: iQOO tipped to be working on a compact gaming tablet
description: iQOO could be gearing up to launch a compact gaming tablet in the coming months, according to a new leak. According to tipster Digital Chat Station, iQOO's upcoming compact gaming tablet will be powered by Qualcomm's next-generation flagship Snapdragon chipset, although the exact SoC wasn't specified. iQOO Pad 6 Pro The tipster also claims the tablet will debut in the second half of the year. It is expected to launch alongside one of iQOO's flagship smartphones, likely the rumored iQOO 16, which is tipped to arrive in September. The gaming tablet is likely to feature a display...

title: Nothing Phone (4b) live images show all three colors, confirm key specs
description: Just a few hours ago, Nothing confirmed that its upcoming Phone (4b) will be powered by a Snapdragon chipset, without going so far as to reveal which one. Meanwhile, an X user seems to have gotten his hands on all three colors of the Nothing Phone (4b) and decided to show them off to the world in live image form. Here they are in all their glory: Nothing Phone (4b) live images The same source has also shared images showing the About Phone section, which confirms a bunch of things about the device. It is running Nothing OS 4.1 based on Android 16. Nothing Phone (4b)...

title: WhatsApp username reservations go live this week
description: WhatsApp is finally, after years of testing and anticipation, introducing usernames. You will be able to reserve yours starting "this week". The usernames themselves will only roll out "later this year" though, in a gradual manner. WhatsApp will notify you when they're available in your country. Obviously, with over three billion users, a lot of people will want the same username, so it's going to be a wild ride. WhatsApp says it's adding usernames because "a phone number is personal and it's tied to so many parts of your life", and "sometimes you just want to chat without handing over...

title: Comcast is spinning off NBCUniversal and Sky
description: Comcast is spinning off NBCUniversal and Sky into a new company. The split will leave Comcast with the cable, wireless, and business services, while the new company will handle the Universal theme parks, film and TV studios, NBC, Peacock, and Sky. This move practically undoes the 2011 acquisition of NBCUniversal by Comcast. So after 15 years of consolidation, it's now time for some de-consolidation. Interestingly, Comcast CEO Brian Roberts will remain "actively involved" in leading both companies after the split. Roberts, whose father Ralph founded the company in 1963, controls...

title: BT and Verizon agree to combine their international enterprise operations
description: Verizon and BT Group have announced plans to combine their international enterprise fixed-line operations into a new joint venture. The move is expected to close in 2027, subject to regulatory approval. BT and Verizon say the new company will be "designed for a cloud-first world in the age of AI", whatever that means. It will be a 50-50 joint venture, and will serve over 3,000 multinational organizations in more than 180 countries. Verizon will pay BT an equalization amount of $625 million to guarantee even voting rights. Combining BT and Verizon’s international enterprise...

title: Motorola Razr Fold review
T-Mobile is retiring old plans and forcing people onto its newest ones
description: T-Mobile's doing some spring cleaning this summer. The company has decided to phase out some older plans and move the customers using them to its newest plans. The old plans that are affected are the oldest ones still in use by T-Mobile customers, and some of them are 15 years old. According to T-Mobile, this will involve price hikes, but the average hike is apparently only going to be $4 per line. Customers on the very old plans will be contacted directly by T-Mobile via email with two weeks' notice before the change takes place on their next billing cycle in mid-July. Here's the...

title: iPhone 18, iPhone 18e, and iPhone Air 2 screen specs leak
description: A new rumor from Digital Chat Station on Weibo today brings us details about the early 2027 iPhone lineup. As you may already know, Apple is expected to launch the iPhone 18 Pro, iPhone 18 Pro Max, and iPhone Ultra this September. Then, in spring 2027, perhaps in April, the company is rumored to unveil the iPhone 18, the iPhone 18e, and the iPhone Air 2. According to DCS, the iPhone 18 will have a 6.3-inch LTPO OLED screen with "1.5K" resolution and 120Hz refresh rate, the iPhone 18e will have a 6.12-inch LTPS OLED screen with "1.5K" resolution and 60Hz refresh rate, and the iPhone Air 2...

title: New report reveals when Samsung might be launching the first rollable-screen smartphone
description: Rollable-screen smartphones have been a dream for years, and yet we still don’t have any to buy. That might change in a couple of years or so, if a new report from Korea is to be believed. According to unnamed industry sources, Samsung Display wants to be the king of the rollable screen market, as it's feeling a lot of pressure from Chinese display panel makers in the foldable space. As a consequence, Samsung Display is allegedly in discussions with Samsung Electronics to supply the latter with rollable screens for a smartphone that is currently targeting a release in the first half of...

title: vivo X Fold6 will reportedly see a global launch
description: vivo unveiled its latest flagship foldable, the vivo X Fold6, in China last week. While the company didn’t mention an international release during the launch, it has now confirmed that the device will make its way to global markets. In a statement to Android Authority, vivo confirmed that the X Fold6 will launch globally. The company did not reveal a launch timeline or the list of markets, but said that more details regarding availability will be announced soon. Notably, the vivo X Fold5 launched in China before making its way to select Southeast Asian markets. Whether the X...

title: Nothing confirms Snapdragon chip for the Phone (4b)
description: Nothing is launching the Phone (4b) on July 7, and the company revealed its design last week. It's very obviously a Nothing phone, but it is also quite clearly the lowest-end of the bunch. Today Nothing has revealed that the Phone (4b) will be powered by a Snapdragon chipset. Unfortunately, it hasn't actually specified which one yet. However, the phone was spotted in the Geekbench database a few days ago, and that listing revealed that it will be powered by the Snapdragon 6 Gen 4 SoC, paired with 8GB of RAM. The Nothing Phone (4b) will be running Android 16 out of the box. According...

title: Amazfit launches a beta feature for iOS notification forwarding
description: Amazfit has been working on delivering better notifications from your iPhone to its smart wearables. The company now officially announced the start of a beta testing program for its iOS Notification Forwarding feature. But there's a catch - the beta access is available only to users located in the EU. The new notification handling allows not just basic alerts, but also supports interaction with compatible apps. You can now view entire messages, reply, send a quick response or mark as read directly on your smartwatch. The integration works with supported apps, like WhatsApp. Image...

title: Foldable iPhone Ultra dummy appears in black finish
description: Apple is expected to unveil its first foldable iPhone alongside the iPhone 18 Pro series this September. Rumored to be called the iPhone Ultra, the device has now surfaced in a fresh set of leaked dummy images. While dummy units of the foldable have surfaced before, this latest leak offers a look at the device in a black finish. The images show its wide-screen form factor, similar to the Huawei Pura X Max. According to the leak, the iPhone Ultra could be nearly as thin as the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold7 when folded and measure just 4.5mm when unfolded. The rear houses a dual-camera...

title: Leak suggests Xiaomi 18 will get only a minor battery upgrade
description: New details about Xiaomi's upcoming compact flagship, the Xiaomi 18, have surfaced ahead of its rumored launch in China this September. According to tipster Digital Chat Station, the Xiaomi 18 will pack a 7,200mAh battery in China. Xiaomi 18 render courtesy of Digital Chat Station That’s only a modest upgrade over the 7,000mAh battery found in the Chinese version of the Xiaomi 17. The global Xiaomi 17, however, shipped with a smaller 6,330mAh battery. It is, therefore, likely that the global variant of the Xiaomi 18 could feature a smaller battery than its Chinese...

title: Samsung Galaxy M47 goes official with SD 6 Gen 3 and 6,000mAh battery
description: As expected, Samsung launched its Galaxy M47 in India today. The latest M-series member brings some familiar looks and specs to the Galaxy A27, but it also features some upgrades. The main one is the larger 6,000mAh battery, which supports up to 45W wired charging. It also features bypass charging, allowing the device to sip power directly from the charger when plugged in. This is highly useful for gamers and helps keep the device cooler. Elsewhere, the M47 is quite similar to the A27. It features a 6.7-inch Super AMOLED display with FHD+ resolution and a 120Hz refresh rate. The...

title: RedMagic Gaming Tablet 5 Pro confirmed for global markets as Astra 2
description: RedMagic's Gaming Tablet 5 Pro is set to launch in China tomorrow, and the company has now confirmed that the tablet will be making its way to global markets. As expected, it will launch internationally under the RedMagic Astra 2 branding. While RedMagic has yet to announce a global launch date, the Astra 2 is expected to retain the same specifications as its Chinese counterpart. The Gaming Tablet 5 Pro is confirmed to feature a 9.06-inch OLED display with a 2.4K resolution and a 185Hz refresh rate. It will be powered by the Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 chipset and equipped with a...
